messages.content:
SQL dialect: duckdb

SCHEMA:
Table: analytics.customers
  One row per customer.
  - customer_id (bigint) — Primary key.
  - name (text) — Customer display name.
  - email (text) — Contact email.
  - region (text) — Sales region.
  - plan (text) — Subscription tier.
  - signup_date (date) — Date the customer signed up.
  join -> analytics.orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id

Table: analytics.orders
  One row per confirmed order. Excludes carts and draft orders.
  - order_id (bigint) — Primary key.
  - customer_id (bigint) — FK to analytics.customers.customer_id.
  - amount_cents (bigint) — Order total in cents. Divide by 100 for dollars.
  - status (text) — One of 'confirmed','refunded','partial_refund'.
  - created_at (timestamp) — Order confirmation time (UTC).
  measure net_revenue: SUM(CASE WHEN status='confirmed' THEN amount_cents ELSE 0 END)/100.0
  filter last_quarter: created_at >= date_trunc('quarter', now()) - interval '3 months'
  join -> analytics.customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.customer_id

COVERAGE SEEDS (aim to exercise these):
- [easy] table:analytics.customers: a simple count or single-table aggregate over analytics.customers
- [hard] join:analytics.customers->analytics.orders: a breakdown that joins analytics.customers to analytics.orders on customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id
- [easy] table:analytics.orders: a simple count or single-table aggregate over analytics.orders
- [medium] measure:analytics.orders.net_revenue: use the measure net_revenue := SUM(CASE WHEN status='confirmed' THEN amount_cents ELSE 0 END)/100.0
- [medium] filter:analytics.orders.last_quarter: apply the named filter last_quarter := created_at >= date_trunc('quarter', now()) - interval '3 months'
- [hard] join:analytics.orders->analytics.customers: a breakdown that joins analytics.orders to analytics.customers on orders.customer_id = customers.customer_id

Produce about 7 candidate questions with gold SQL, spread across the seeds and difficulty levels, including a few phrasing variants. Return them all.

DraftQuestion.description: One model-proposed question + gold SQL, before it has been executed.
DraftQuestion.title: DraftQuestion

response_schema.description: The structured result of the single paid drafting call.
response_schema.title: DraftBatch

request.system: You author evaluation questions for a text-to-SQL benchmark. For each question you also write the GOLD SQL that answers it. Rules: (1) every query must be a single read-only SELECT in the given SQL dialect; (2) use the fully-qualified table names and real columns from the schema — never invent columns; (3) each gold query must actually return rows against real data (avoid impossible filters); (4) exercise the provided measures, named filters, and joins — that is the business logic worth testing; (5) for some canonical questions, add one or two phrasing VARIANTS that ask the same thing differently, sharing a `group` slug with their canonical question and setting canonical=false. Stratify difficulty across easy/medium/hard.

questions.gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM analytics.orders
questions.question: Total order count please

questions.gold_sql: SELECT c.region, SUM(o.amount_cents)/100.0 AS r FROM analytics.orders o JOIN analytics.customers c ON o.customer_id=c.customer_id GROUP BY c.region
questions.question: Revenue by region
